title: G.V. Black Classification Sorter
sourceNote: Original scenarios and diagrams authored from course-verified lesion locations in REHE 162. No course slide artwork is reproduced.

classes.title: Class I
classes.rule: Pits and fissures on occlusal surfaces, or in buccal or lingual pits.
classes.contrast: Location in a pit or fissure is the deciding feature.

classes.title: Class II
classes.rule: Proximal surfaces of premolars and molars.
classes.contrast: The posterior proximal location defines the class; a generic smooth-surface label does not.

classes.title: Class III
classes.rule: Proximal surfaces of anterior teeth without incisal-edge involvement.
classes.contrast: If the incisal edge is involved, sort the lesion as Class IV instead.

classes.title: Class IV
classes.rule: A proximal surface of an anterior tooth with incisal-edge involvement.
classes.contrast: Both the anterior proximal surface and the incisal edge are involved.

classes.title: Class V
classes.rule: The gingival or cervical third of a facial or lingual surface.
classes.contrast: The cervical-third surface location is the deciding feature.

classes.title: Class VI
classes.rule: A posterior cusp-tip lesion, or a lesion confined to the incisal edge of an anterior tooth.
classes.contrast: An anterior example stays on the incisal edge and does not extend onto a proximal surface.

cases.prompt: A discrete lesion follows the central pit-and-fissure system on a molar's occlusal surface.
cases.clinicalCue: Posterior tooth · occlusal pits and fissures
cases.explanation: Class I includes lesions in pits and fissures, including those on an occlusal surface.

cases.prompt: A small lesion is confined to a buccal pit on a molar.
cases.explanation: A lesion limited to a buccal pit is Class I because the course rule includes buccal pits.

cases.prompt: A small lesion is confined to a lingual pit on a posterior tooth.
cases.explanation: A lesion limited to a lingual pit is Class I because the course rule includes lingual pits.

cases.prompt: A lesion is localized to the mesial proximal surface of a premolar.
cases.explanation: Proximal lesions on premolars and molars are Class II.

cases.prompt: A lesion is localized to the distal proximal surface of a molar.
cases.explanation: The posterior proximal location places this lesion in Class II.

cases.prompt: A lesion sits on a premolar's proximal surface directly beneath the contact area.
cases.explanation: A premolar is a posterior tooth, so its proximal-surface lesion is Class II.

cases.prompt: An incisor has a mesial proximal lesion. The incisal edge remains intact and is not involved.
cases.clinicalCue: Anterior tooth · proximal surface · incisal edge not involved
cases.explanation: An anterior proximal lesion without incisal-edge involvement is Class III.

cases.prompt: A lateral incisor has a distal proximal lesion that stops short of the uninvolved incisal edge.
cases.clinicalCue: Anterior tooth · distal proximal surface · incisal edge not involved
cases.explanation: Because the lesion is anterior and proximal but does not involve the incisal edge, it is Class III.

cases.prompt: The marked lesion is limited to an incisor's proximal surface; the full incisal edge is visibly uninvolved.
cases.clinicalCue: Anterior tooth · proximal only · incisal edge intact
cases.explanation: Proximal-only involvement on an anterior tooth is Class III when the incisal edge is spared.

cases.prompt: An incisor's mesial proximal lesion extends through the mesioincisal corner and involves the incisal edge.
cases.clinicalCue: Anterior tooth · proximal surface plus incisal edge
cases.explanation: Anterior proximal involvement plus incisal-edge involvement defines Class IV.

cases.prompt: A lateral incisor's distal proximal lesion reaches the distoincisal corner and includes the incisal edge.
cases.clinicalCue: Anterior tooth · distal proximal plus incisal edge
cases.explanation: Once an anterior proximal lesion includes the incisal edge, it is Class IV rather than Class III.

cases.prompt: The marked anterior lesion spans a proximal surface and the adjoining incisal edge.
cases.clinicalCue: Anterior tooth · proximal and incisal-edge involvement
cases.explanation: The combined proximal and incisal-edge location is the Class IV boundary.

cases.prompt: A lesion is confined to the cervical third of a premolar's facial surface.
cases.explanation: A lesion in the gingival or cervical third of a facial surface is Class V.

cases.prompt: A lesion is confined to the cervical third of a molar's lingual surface.
cases.explanation: Class V includes the cervical third on either a facial or lingual surface.

cases.prompt: A lesion is centered in the cervical third of an incisor's facial surface.
cases.explanation: The cervical-third facial location makes this Class V regardless of the anterior tooth category.

cases.prompt: A lesion is confined to the tip of a premolar cusp.
cases.explanation: A posterior cusp-tip lesion is Class VI.

cases.prompt: A lesion is confined to one cusp tip on a molar.
cases.explanation: A lesion isolated to a posterior cusp tip is Class VI, not an occlusal pit-and-fissure Class I lesion.

cases.prompt: A lesion is confined to the center of an incisor's incisal edge and does not involve either proximal surface.
cases.clinicalCue: Anterior tooth · incisal edge only · proximal surfaces not involved
cases.explanation: An anterior lesion confined to the incisal edge is Class VI; proximal extension would change the boundary.
